title: BEM-928 analytics report
objective:
Create file governance/reports/BEM928_DIALOGUE_ANALYTICS_REPORT.md with content # BEM-928 DIALOGUE ANALYTICS REPORT
Status: DIALOGUE_ANALYTICS_REPORT_READY
Date: 2026-05-31
Purpose: analytical report based on yesterday's dialogue descriptions, organized by themes with problem, discussion, accepted decisions and protocol implications.
Correction applied:
- User requested not another implementation protocol, but an analytical report of the dialogues.
- Report is structured by thematic dialogues and focuses on: problem solved, what was discussed, accepted decision, and what must be transferred into the final protocol.
Key sections:
- Executive summary.
- Chronology of thematic dialogues.
- Detailed analysis of dialogue themes.
- Consolidated problems and decisions.
- Checklist of decisions that must not be lost.
- What the future final protocol must include.
- Final analytical position and completeness check.
Major decisions captured:
- Objects GD/DIR/WRK have passports, not prompts.
- Prompts belong only to LLM roles, providers and operational handlers.
- Curator is an object-level element.
- Internal contour algorithm is Curator -> Analyst -> Auditor -> Executor -> Auditor -> Curator with revision through Analyst.
- Vertical links are curator-to-curator.
- Horizontal links are Auditor A -> Analyst B or Analyst B requests verified data from Auditor A.
- Workspace belongs to internal contour.
- No local individual element rules by default.
- Provider model is GPT Analyst, Claude Auditor, Claude Executor, fallback GPT-Codex/Codex.
- Telegram is an operator front, not the center of the system.
- Claude requirements are release blockers.
Local operator artifacts:
- BEM928_dialogue_analytics_report_ru.docx
- BEM928_dialogue_analytics_report_ru.zip
QA: DOCX rendered to 8 pages; no edge clipping detected.
No issue comments.
